title: Annual deforestation by region (2010–2014)
subtitle: Annual deforestation is measured as the average between 2010 and 2014, and is measured in hectares.
caption:
Annual deforestation by region (2010–2014)

Annual deforestation is measured as the average between 2010 and 2014, and is measured in hectares.

This horizontal bar chart displays average annual deforestation figures for seven distinct geographic regions and countries between 2010 and 2014, with values measured in hectares. Brazil occupies the top position with the longest bar, representing 1.71 million hectares of deforestation. The category labeled "Americas (excl. Brazil and Mexico)" follows with the second-highest value at 1.08 million hectares. Africa ranks third with 902,219 hectares, followed closely by Indonesia at 720,694 hectares. The "Asia and Pacific (excl. Indonesia and India)" group accounts for 579,360 hectares. The bottom two entries show significantly lower figures compared to the top regions: Mexico is listed at 103,957 hectares, and India has the shortest bar on the chart at 73,018 hectares. The visual arrangement highlights a steep gradient, where the top two entries exceed one million hectares, while the values drop below one million for the subsequent regions and fall below 200,000 hectares for the final two entries. All bars are rendered in a uniform blue color against a white background, with specific numerical labels placed at the end of each bar to indicate the precise average area lost.
